window.label: Entire history
window.phrase: the entire available history up to 2026-07-22

evidence.claim: On July 22, 2024, DP World formally commenced operations at Dar es Salaam Port under a 30-year concession agreement.
evidence.snippet: DP World took over cargo handling at multi-purpose berths, promising massive investments to improve port efficiency and reduce dwell times.

evidence.claim: On August 19, 2024, Masdar signed a joint development agreement with TANESCO to build a 2 GW solar and wind energy project.
evidence.snippet: The ambitious agreement aims to rapidly expand Tanzania's clean energy generation capacity, addressing domestic grid reliability issues.

evidence.claim: On October 28, 2024, Flydubai expanded its East African presence by launching four weekly direct flights to Zanzibar.
evidence.snippet: The additional flights will support tourism and trade exchanges between the UAE and Tanzania's autonomous archipelago.

evidence.claim: On November 12, 2024, AD Ports Group signed a concession agreement to develop and manage regional container terminals in Tanzania.
evidence.snippet: The long-term deal covers modernizing secondary ports in Tanzania, boosting coastal shipping trade across the East African Community.

evidence.claim: On September 14, 2024, UAE's ADQ signed an MoU with Tanzania's government to pursue massive investments in logistics and agribusiness.
evidence.snippet: ADQ will explore commercial farming projects and logistics networks to link Tanzanian agricultural produce directly with Gulf markets.

evidence.claim: On December 15, 2024, the UAE-Tanzania Business Forum in Dubai celebrated a record $2.5 billion in annual bilateral trade volume.
evidence.snippet: Bilateral trade has risen dramatically, driven by gold exports, mineral trade, and agricultural shipments to the UAE.

evidence.claim: On October 5, 2024, UAE-based Al Rawabi Group entered a joint venture for mineral and gold exploration in western Tanzania.
evidence.snippet: The mining agreement involves modern exploration technology deployments and funding for large-scale gold processing operations.

evidence.claim: On January 15, 2025, UAE telecom giant e& held negotiations to acquire a 30% stake in a major Tanzanian mobile operator.
evidence.snippet: The proposed acquisition aligns with e&'s strategic expansion plans across high-growth African telecom and mobile money markets.

evidence.claim: On November 5, 2024, the Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation completed 10 solar-powered water well projects in rural Tanzania.
evidence.snippet: These clean water wells will serve approximately 25,000 residents in drought-prone areas, improving agricultural yields and health.

evidence.claim: On February 18, 2025, ADFD approved a $50 million soft loan to upgrade the Chalinze-Morogoro highway section in eastern Tanzania.
evidence.snippet: The infrastructure project will dual the existing single-carriageway road to improve traffic flow along a major transit corridor.

edges.claim: On July 22, 2024, DP World formally commenced operations at Dar es Salaam Port under a 30-year concession agreement.
On August 19, 2024, Masdar signed a joint development agreement with TANESCO to build a 2 GW solar and wind energy project.
On October 5, 2024, UAE-based Al Rawabi Group entered a joint venture for mineral and gold exploration in western Tanzania.
On January 15, 2025, UAE telecom giant e& held negotiations to acquire a 30% stake in a major Tanzanian mobile operator.
